original: Astronomers at the University of California witness the first sight of the birth of a galaxy
Sherlock Holmes, British fictional detective (created by Arthur Conan Doyle), "born" in London (d. c. 1893)
New Study discovers ancient Roman concrete is stronger than modern concrete because of the use of a hot mixing technique, with quicklime that self-healed any cracks over time
"Schoolhouse Rock" premieres on ABC-TV with Multiplication Rock
The beginning of the first GPS epoch
Maria Montessoriopens her 1st Montessori school, in Rome
Geophysicist and meteorologistAlfred Wegenerpresents his controversial theory of continental drift in a lecture at the Geological Association at the Senckenberg-Museum, Frankfurt
NHL Philadelphia Flyers set North American professional sports record of 35 straight games without a defeat (25-0-10)
Jacques-Étienne Montgolfier, French inventor and pioneer balloonist (Montgolfière-style hot air balloon), born in Annonay, France (d. 1799)
1st postage stamp copyrighted by US (Carl Sandburg stamp)
Qian Hong swims female world record 50m butterfly (27.30 sec)
Australian cricket iconDon Bradmanscores 452 not out for New South Wales v Queensland in Sydney; then world record individual score in a first-class match; runs scored in 415 minutes
1st telephone message from a submerged submarine, by Simon Lake
Samuel Morseand Alfred Vail demonstrate their telegraph machine in New Jersey
Surveyor 7 (last of series) launched by US for soft-landing on Moon
Record snow cover in Seattle - 120 cm
Record $3.1 million (333.6 million yen) price for giant bluefin tuna at Tokyo's Toyosu fish market bought by sushi restaurant owner Kiyoshi Kimura
Leah Chase 'Queen of Creole Cuisine', New Orleans chef and author, born in New Orleans, Louisiana (d. 2019)
Earl Scruggs, American bluegrass banjo player (Flatt & Scruggs - "Foggy Mountain Breakdown"; "Ballad of Jed Clampett"), born in Flint Hill, North Carolina (d. 2012)
Cheng Pei Pei, Hong Kong actress, cinema's 1st female action star' (Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon), born in Shanghai, China[1]
Alfred, King of Wessex, is surprised by a nighttime attack in Chippenham, by the Great Viking Army led by Guthrum the Dane. Alfred escapes into the Somerset marshes.
Berkeley chemists announces 1st synthetic growth hormones
Pan American Airlines becomes the first commercial airline to complete a flight around the world; the plane "California Clipper" is rechristened "Pacific Clipper" after the feat
US PresidentFranklin Rooseveltdelivers his "Four Freedoms" speech (freedom of speech and worship; freedom from want and fear) during his US State of the Union address
US figure skating championNancy Kerriganis attacked byTonya Harding's bodyguard at the US Championships in Detroit, Michigan
Paavo Nurmi, sets indoor record, 4:13.6 mile & 14:44.6 5,000m
Indianapolis Olympians beat Rochester Royals, 75-73 in NBA-record 6 overtimes; also the longest game in NBA history
The Beatles' album "Magical Mystery Tour" goes #1 and stays #1 for 8 weeks
Village People's "Y.M.C.A." becomes their only UK #1 single; at its peak, it sells over 150,000 copies per day
Jean Mueller discovers comet Mueller/1993a
Record £42 million ($65.2 million) British lottery won by 3 people (2-3-4-13-42-44)
Tennessee Titans quarterback Marcus Mariota scores a touchdown off own pass in 22-21 victory over Kansas City Chiefs, second NFL quarterback to do so
Kate Edger, First woman in New Zealand and probably the second in the British Empire to gain a university degree (Batchelor of Arts), born in Abingdon, England (d. 1935)
Harry Pace, American music label owner (Black Swan - 1st major label founded by a Black American), born in Covington, Georgia (d. 1943)
2 day storm off Irish & English coast immortalized as the "Big Wind"
Clement Reid, English geologist and paleobotanist, first to suggest a drowned land bridge between Britain and Europe (now called Doggerland), born in London (d. 1916)
Khalil Gibran, Lebanese-American poet (The Prophet; Broken Wings; Sand and Foam), painter, and philosopher, born in Bsharri, Mount Lebanon, Ottoman Syria (d. 1931)
Virginia orders half of its tobacco crop destroyed to support plunging prices and avoid an economic catastrophe, the first colony to order the destruction of crops
US hand over St Stephan crown to Hungary
First recorded boxing match is organized by Christopher Monck, 2nd Duke of Albemarle, between his butler and his butcher
Challenger moves to Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida) for mating of STS 41 B mission
"Star Wars: The Force Awakens" breaks North American box office record, passing the $760.5M taken by "Avatar"
Large winter storm caused by a polar vortex moves east across the US from the central plains to the mid Atlantic, bringing 18 inches of snow to Kansas
Mother Teresaarrives in Calcutta to begin her work amongst India's poorest
NaturalistsAlexander von HumboldtandAimé Bonplandreach Quito, Ecuador
Melchora Aquino, Filipino revolutionary hero, born in Caloocan, Philippines (d. 1919)
Murray Rose, Australian swimmer (Olympic gold 400m freestyle 1956, 60; 1500m, 4x200m 1956), born in Birmingham, England (d. 2012)
Ebo Taylor, Ghanaian highlife and Afrobeat musician and composer, born in Cape Coast, Gold Coast
Adriano Celentano, Italian pop-rock singer-songwriter ("24.000 baci"), actor (Serafino), and filmmaker, born in Greco, Milan, Italy
"Wheel Of Fortune" debuts on NBC-TV
Pan American Airlines offers a round-the-world-ticket, the first commercial airline to do so
"Mutual of Omaha's Wild Kingdom" with Marlin Perkins begins on NBC
KHTV TV channel 39 in Houston, TX (IND) begins broadcasting

Australian boxer Johnny Famechon retains his WBC featherweight title with a 14th-round knockout of Fighting Harada in Tokyo; re-match of questionable 15-round decision in 1969 in his first title defense
Last day of Test cricket for Chappell, Marsh & Lillee
Rolling Stones' 1st tour of England as headline act: The Ronettes and The Swinging Blue Jeans open for them
Ferrand, Count of Flanders, freed from the Louvre after being held prisoner for 12 years by the French
Massachusetts slaves petition legislature for freedom
Battle of Jersey: Major Francis Peirson and his men defeat invading French troops
New England Anti-Slavery Society organizes (Boston)
